title: RudderStack Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**RudderStack** is an open-source, warehouse-first Customer Data Platform. It exposes two distinct API surfaces, and this connector — `RudderstackConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "rudderstack"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps both:

| Surface | Base URL | Auth | What it does |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Data Plane** (HTTP API) | `https://hosted.rudderlabs.com` | HTTP **Basic** — `write_key` as username, empty password | Ingests user events: `track`, `identify`, `page`, `screen`, `group`, `alias`, `batch` |
| **Control Plane** (Management API v2) | `https://api.rudderstack.com/v2` | **Bearer** `<personal_access_token>` | Manages Sources, Destinations, Connections, Workspaces, Profiles, Identities |

Methods exposed by the connector:

| Group | Methods |
|---|---|
| Lifecycle | `install`, `authorize`, `health_check`, `sync` |
| Sources | `list_sources`, `get_source`, `create_source` |
| Destinations | `list_destinations`, `get_destination` |
| Connections | `list_connections` |
| Workspaces | `list_workspaces` |
| Profiles / Identities | `list_profiles`, `get_profile`, `list_identities` |
| Events (data plane) | `track_event`, `identify_user`, `page_event`, `screen_event`, `group_event`, `alias_user`, `batch_events` |

The connector normalises control-plane Sources + Destinations into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), exposes one standalone `async def` per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429 / 5xx with exponential backoff + jitter (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::RudderstackHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Get a Write Key (data plane)

1. Sign in to https://app.rudderstack.com (or your self-hosted control plane).
2. **Sources → Add Source** (e.g. JavaScript / HTTP / Python) — RudderStack provisions a source.
3. Open the source — the **Write Key** is shown at the top. Copy it.

### 2. (Optional) Generate a Personal Access Token (control plane)

The PAT is required only for control-plane methods (`list_sources`, `create_source`, `list_destinations`, `list_workspaces`, etc.). For pure event ingestion, skip this step.

1. **Settings → Personal Access Tokens → Generate Token**.
2. Give it `read` / `write` scopes for the resources you want to manage.
3. Copy the token — RudderStack shows it only once.

### 3. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → RudderStack**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `write_key` (secret, **required**) | Source Write Key from step 1 |
| `access_token` (secret, optional) | PAT from step 2 — enables control-plane methods |
| `data_plane_url` (text, optional) | Default `https://hosted.rudderlabs.com`. Override only for self-hosted / private regions. |
| `control_plane_url` (text, optional) | Default `https://api.rudderstack.com/v2`. Override only if you self-host the control plane. |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 100. |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `write_key` is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `RudderstackHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 4. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()`. With a PAT it performs `GET /sources?limit=1` as a lightweight probe. Without a PAT it returns HEALTHY when `write_key` is present (the data plane has no idempotent GET to probe).

| Probe result | Connector status |
|---|---|
| 2xx | `HEALTHY` + `CONNECTED` |
| 401 | `OFFLINE` + `TOKEN_EXPIRED` |
| 403 | `UNHEALTHY` + `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` |
| 429 | `DEGRADED` + `CONNECTED` (logged) |

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
RudderStack uses **two different auth schemes** depending on the surface — both are configured at install time and never refreshed.

### Credential model

| Field | Used by | Header |
|---|---|---|
| `write_key` | Data plane | `Authorization: Basic <base64(write_key + ":")>` |
| `access_token` | Control plane | `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` |

The write_key is sent as the HTTP Basic **username** with an **empty password** — the Segment-compatible auth scheme RudderStack adopted. This is a gotcha: a typical Basic-auth library expects user+pass; here you must explicitly base64 the literal `write_key:` (note the trailing colon).

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `write_key`. Initialises `RudderstackHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any RudderStack endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured `write_key` — there is no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | Control-plane probe `GET /sources?limit=1` when PAT is set; otherwise HEALTHY-when-write_key-present. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None — secrets live in the connector config (encrypted at rest via the platform's credential layer). |

### Header contract per surface

```
# Control plane (Bearer)
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json

# Data plane (Basic)
Authorization: Basic <base64(write_key + ":")>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `write_key` | secret | **yes** | `self.write_key` | HTTP Basic username for data plane |
| `access_token` | secret | no | `self.access_token` | Bearer PAT for control plane |
| `data_plane_url` | text | no | `self.data_plane_url` (default `https://hosted.rudderlabs.com`) | Data plane base URL |
| `control_plane_url` | text | no | `self.control_plane_url` (default `https://api.rudderstack.com/v2`) | Control plane base URL |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 100) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
CONNECTOR_TYPE = "rudderstack"
AUTH_TYPE      = "api_key"
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["write_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DATA_PLANE_BASE    = "https://hosted.rudderlabs.com"
_CONTROL_PLANE_BASE = "https://api.rudderstack.com/v2"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `RudderstackConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::RudderstackHTTPClient`; payload shaping in `helpers/utils.py::normalize_event_payload`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validates `write_key`. Persists config via `save_config()`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a TokenInfo whose `access_token = write_key` for interface symmetry.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
If PAT present → `GET /sources?limit=1`. Otherwise HEALTHY when `write_key` is set. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates control-plane Sources + Destinations → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. When PAT is absent, returns `SyncResult(COMPLETED, message="…")` — RudderStack is event-streaming, there is no event corpus to backfill.

children.title: Sources
children.content:
### `async list_sources(limit=50, after=None) -> Dict`
`GET /sources?limit={N}&after={cursor}`.

### `async get_source(source_id) -> Dict`
`GET /sources/{source_id}`.

### `async create_source(name, type, config=None) -> Dict`
`POST /sources` — body: `{name, type, config}`. `type` is the RudderStack source type (e.g. `Javascript`, `Python`, `HTTP`).

children.title: Workspaces
children.content:
### `async list_workspaces() -> Dict`
`GET /workspaces` — list workspaces accessible to the PAT. Used to scope subsequent calls when an account has multiple workspaces.

children.title: Profiles + Identities
children.content:
### `async list_profiles(limit=50, after=None) -> Dict`
`GET /profiles` — list unified user profiles built by the RudderStack Profiles API.

### `async get_profile(profile_id) -> Dict`
`GET /profiles/{profile_id}`.

### `async list_identities(limit=50, after=None) -> Dict`
`GET /identities` — list raw identity records (one profile can have many identities — email, anonId, …).

children.title: Data-Plane Events
children.content:
Every method below sends HTTP Basic with `write_key` and accepts an optional per-call `write_key` override (multi-source tenants).

### `async track_event(user_id, event, properties=None, write_key=None, timestamp=None) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/track` — body: `{userId, event, properties, timestamp, sentAt}`.

### `async identify_user(user_id, traits=None, write_key=None, timestamp=None) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/identify` — body: `{userId, traits, timestamp, sentAt}`.

### `async page_event(user_id, name=None, properties=None, write_key=None, timestamp=None) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/page` — body: `{userId, name?, properties, timestamp, sentAt}`.

### `async screen_event(user_id, name=None, properties=None, write_key=None, timestamp=None) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/screen` — mobile equivalent of page.

### `async group_event(user_id, group_id, traits=None, write_key=None, timestamp=None) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/group` — body: `{userId, groupId, traits, timestamp, sentAt}`.

### `async alias_user(user_id, previous_id, write_key=None, timestamp=None) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/alias` — body: `{userId, previousId, timestamp, sentAt}`.

### `async batch_events(events, write_key=None) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/batch` — body: `{batch: [...events], sentAt}`. Each entry must be a fully-formed event with a `type` field (`track` / `identify` / `page` / `screen` / `group` / `alias`).

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
RudderstackError                       # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── RudderstackAuthError               # 401 / 403
├── RudderstackBadRequestError         # 400
├── RudderstackNotFoundError           # 404 — carries resource_id
├── RudderstackConflictError           # 409
├── RudderstackRateLimitError          # 429 — retry_after_s
└── RudderstackServerError             # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:

```
RudderstackNetworkError = RudderstackServerError    # legacy name
RudderstackNotFound     = RudderstackNotFoundError
RudderStack* = Rudderstack*                          # old capitalisation
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff (`RETRY_DELAY_S * BACKOFF_FACTOR ** attempt + jitter`, capped at `MAX_RETRY_DELAY_S`), retry up to `DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `RudderstackServerError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED) with the exception message
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `write_key` blank | Paste the Source Write Key from RudderStack → Sources → your source. |
| `RudderstackAuthError("access_token is required for control-plane calls")` when calling `list_sources` etc. | No PAT configured | Generate a Personal Access Token in RudderStack → Settings → Personal Access Tokens, paste into the `access_token` install field. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from RudderStack — PAT lacks scope | In RudderStack Dashboard → Settings → PAT, edit and grant `read:sources` (and `write:*` for create/update). |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — PAT revoked or write_key wrong | Rotate the PAT, and verify the write_key matches the source you're sending events to. |
| Events return 200 but never appear in destination | Wrong `write_key` for the source bound to that destination | Each source has its own write_key. Use the per-call `write_key=` override when sending to multiple sources from one tenant. |
| `rudderstack.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (RudderStack per-minute rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff + jitter. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch events via `batch_events`. |
| 404 on `get_source("…")` | Wrong `source_id` or source deleted | Call `list_sources()` to confirm IDs. RudderStack source IDs look like `2Lb…` (Base62). |
| `RudderstackAuthError` on `track_event` with status 401 | Bad write_key (or no source matches it) | Verify the write_key. Note RudderStack sends `write_key` as Basic-auth **username** with empty password — a typical `Authorization: Bearer <write_key>` is wrong. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'RudderStackHTTPClient'` | Older `client/__init__.py` without back-compat alias | Already fixed — `RudderStackHTTPClient = RudderstackHTTPClient` alias added in `client/__init__.py`. |
| Self-hosted control plane returns 404 on `/v2/sources` | `control_plane_url` doesn't include the `/v2` suffix | Set `control_plane_url` to `https://your-host/v2`, not just `https://your-host`. |
